modification: H intercalated in rocksalt LaO layer at (0.5, 0.5, 0)
space_group: P4/mmm (symmetry lowered from I4/mmm by H ordering)

h_plus_intercalation.effect: Electron doping -- pushes toward Mott insulator
h_plus_intercalation.sc_prospect: POOR: Ni(+2) is insulating, not superconducting

h_minus_intercalation.effect: Hole doping -- pushes away from Mott transition
h_minus_intercalation.sc_prospect: MARGINAL: metallic but weaker correlations at Ni(+3)

half_h_option.note: Partial intercalation could maintain closer-to-optimal doping

doping_analysis.key_finding: H intercalation changes Ni valence; must be carefully controlled to maintain SC state

h_environment.site: tetrahedral interstitial in LaO rocksalt layer
h_environment.bonding: H likely forms O-H bond (0.79 A is typical O-H distance)

concerns: H intercalation changes Ni valence (either +2.0 or +3.0 depending on H charge state)
Optimal Ni valence for SC is +2.5; any H content disrupts this
H diffusion in the rocksalt layer may be fast at T > 200 K
Structural distortion from H ordering may lower symmetry significantly
15 GPa pressure needed for parent SC may not be compatible with H intercalation
